core_behavior: The system is responsible for creating, routing, delivering, storing, and managing notifications to users across one or more delivery channels.
The system must support the following lifecycle states for every notification: CREATED, PENDING, DELIVERED, READ, FAILED, DISMISSED, EXPIRED, ARCHIVED.
CREATED: Notification has been generated and queued.
PENDING: Notification is awaiting delivery.
DELIVERED: Notification has been successfully sent to the channel.
READ: User has acknowledged or opened the notification.
FAILED: Delivery failed after all retry attempts.
DISMISSED: User explicitly dismissed the notification.
EXPIRED: Notification exceeded its TTL without being read.
ARCHIVED: Notification moved to long-term storage.
The system must support the following notification categories: Informational, Warning, Error/Alert, Action Required, Promotional, System.
Informational — General status updates (e.g., 'Your report is ready').
Warning — Conditions requiring user attention (e.g., 'Storage is 90% full').
Error/Alert — Critical conditions requiring immediate action.
Action Required — Notifications that include one or more actionable responses.
Promotional — Marketing or feature-announcement messages (opt-in only).
System — Internal system events (e.g., maintenance windows, version updates).
The system must support delivery via the following channels: In-App, Email, SMS, Push Notification, Webhook.
In-App — Displayed within the application UI (bell icon / notification center).
Email — Sent to the user's registered email address.
SMS — Sent to the user's registered mobile number.
Push Notification — Sent to registered mobile or desktop devices via push service.
Webhook — HTTP POST to a user- or system-configured endpoint.
Each notification may be delivered to one or more channels simultaneously.
Channel selection is governed by notification type, user preferences, and system rules.
Each notification type has a default channel set defined in system configuration.
User preferences override defaults, except for mandatory system notifications.
Mandatory system notifications (e.g., security alerts, account lockouts) are always delivered via all available channels regardless of user preferences.
If a preferred channel is unavailable, the system must fall back to the next available channel in the user's preference order.
Promotional notifications require explicit user opt-in per channel; they must not be sent if no opt-in exists.
Users must be able to view all in-app notifications in a notification center panel.
Notifications must be displayed in reverse-chronological order by default.
Users must be able to filter by: type, read/unread status, date range.
Users must be able to sort by: date, type, priority.
Unread count must be displayed as a badge on the notification icon.
Users must be able to mark individual notifications or all notifications as read.
Users must be able to dismiss (delete) individual notifications or all notifications.
All notification content must be generated from versioned templates.
Templates must support variable interpolation (e.g., {{user.name}}, {{event.timestamp}}).
Templates must be channel-specific (an email template differs from an SMS template for the same event).
Templates must support localization (i18n); the system must select the template matching the user's locale, falling back to the default locale (en-US) if no match exists.
Notifications may be scheduled for future delivery with a specified deliver_at timestamp.
The system must support per-user throttle limits to prevent notification fatigue.
Default throttle limit: no more than 20 notifications per user per hour across all channels.
Throttle limits are configurable per notification type and per channel.
inputs: Notification type (Informational, Warning, Error/Alert, Action Required, Promotional, System).
System configuration defining default channel sets per notification type.
Template variables for interpolation (e.g., user.name, event.timestamp).
Registered mobile or desktop devices for push notification delivery.
Explicit user opt-in per channel for promotional notifications.
outputs: In-app notifications displayed within the application UI (bell icon / notification center).
Email notifications sent to the user's registered email address.
SMS notifications sent to the user's registered mobile number.
Push notifications sent to registered mobile or desktop devices via push service.
HTTP POST to a user- or system-configured webhook endpoint.
Unread count badge displayed on the notification icon.
Error log entry when template rendering fails.
Notification lifecycle state transitions (CREATED, PENDING, DELIVERED, READ, FAILED, DISMISSED, EXPIRED, ARCHIVED).
assumptions: Users have a registered email address for email delivery.
Users have a registered mobile number for SMS delivery.
Users have registered mobile or desktop devices for push notification delivery.
failure_modes: Delivery failed after all retry attempts results in FAILED state.
Template rendering failures must not silently produce partial output; they must trigger a FAILED state with an error log entry.
If a preferred channel is unavailable, the system must fall back to the next available channel in the user's preference order.
edge_cases: Mandatory system notifications are always delivered via all available channels regardless of user preferences.
Promotional notifications must not be sent if no opt-in exists.
If no locale match exists for a template, the system falls back to the default locale (en-US).
Notification exceeded its TTL without being read results in EXPIRED state.
security_constraints: Mandatory system notifications (e.g., security alerts, account lockouts) are always delivered via all available channels regardless of user preferences.
performance_constraints: Default throttle limit: no more than 20 notifications per user per hour across all channels.
Throttle limits are configurable per notification type and per channel.
